title: Night Market Weekend, Every Booth Reviewed

sentences.en: Friday night, our whole family went to the night market near the old bank building, which is basically our weekly tradition.
sentences.zh: 星期五晚上，我們全家去了老銀行大樓附近的夜市，這基本上已經是我們每週的傳統活動。

sentences.en: The first booth sold hamburgers and fries, and William ordered a burger almost as big as his own head.
sentences.zh: 第一個攤位賣漢堡跟薯條，William 點了一個幾乎跟他自己頭一樣大的漢堡。

sentences.en: I picked a small chocolate pudding with a little cream on top, mostly because the sample tasted amazing.
sentences.zh: 我挑了一個小巧克力布丁，上面加了一點鮮奶油，主要是因為試吃的味道太棒了。

sentences.en: Sophia bought a slice of pie and a warm cup of coffee, saying a proper night market trip always needs one sweet and one hot drink.
sentences.zh: Sophia 買了一片派跟一杯溫熱的咖啡，說一趟正式的夜市之旅一定要有一樣甜點跟一杯熱飲。

sentences.en: Dad ordered a plate of salad, which honestly seemed like the wrong food choice for a night market full of fried snacks.
sentences.zh: 爸爸點了一盤沙拉，老實說在滿是油炸小吃的夜市裡，這個選擇聽起來完全不對。

sentences.en: There was also a booth selling honey and small jars of local jam, and the seller let us taste a spoonful for free.
sentences.zh: 還有一個攤位賣蜂蜜跟一小罐一小罐的當地果醬，老闆讓我們免費試吃一小口。

sentences.en: We stopped at a bag of popcorn on sale for a very cheap price, so cheap that Dad bought two bags instead of one.
sentences.zh: 我們在一攤特價的爆米花前停下來，價格便宜到爸爸乾脆買了兩袋而不是一袋。

sentences.en: One stall sold small items like belts, glasses, and pins, and William picked out a pin shaped like a tiny robot head.
sentences.zh: 有一攤賣皮帶、眼鏡跟徽章這類小東西，William 挑了一個小小機器人頭形狀的徽章。

sentences.en: A camera and cellphone accessory booth had a huge sale sign, though Mom said the tax on electronics there always seems suspicious.
sentences.zh: 一攤賣相機跟手機配件的攤位掛著超大特價招牌，不過媽媽說那邊電子產品的稅金聽起來就怪怪的。

sentences.en: There was a small pet shop stall showing off two tiny puppies in a warm box, and William nearly cried when we said we could not bring one home.
sentences.zh: 有一個小小的寵物攤，展示了兩隻窩在暖箱裡的小狗，我們說不能帶回家的時候 William 差點哭出來。

sentences.en: A toy stall sold cheap plastic dolls, small kites, and a whole shelf of items that all looked expensive but definitely were not.
sentences.zh: 一攤玩具店賣便宜的塑膠娃娃、小風箏，還有一整排看起來很貴但其實一點都不貴的東西。

sentences.en: Bernice, who joined us that night, bought a gift for her mother's upcoming birthday, wrapped by the seller for free.
sentences.zh: 那天晚上加入我們的 Bernice，買了一份禮物要送給她媽媽即將到來的生日，老闆還免費幫忙包裝。

sentences.en: She said finding a nice, affordable present at the night market is way easier than searching in any actual shopping mall.
sentences.zh: 她說在夜市找到一份好看又負擔得起的禮物，比在任何一間真正的購物中心找還簡單。

sentences.en: By the end of the night, William's small robot pin was somehow already stuck to three different bags, and no one knew how it got there.
sentences.zh: 夜市逛到最後，William 那個小機器人徽章不知怎麼地已經黏在三個不同的袋子上，沒人知道是怎麼跑過去的。

sentences.en: We walked home with full stomachs and empty wallets, which Sophia said is the honest final score of every good night market trip.
sentences.zh: 我們吃得飽飽的、錢包空空的走回家，Sophia 說這就是每一趟成功夜市之旅老實的最終戰績。

quiz.question: What does William order at the first food booth?
options: A hamburger almost as big as his own head.

quiz.question: What almost makes William cry at the night market?
options: Not being able to bring home a puppy.

quiz.question: According to Sophia, what is the "honest final score" of every good night market trip?
options: A bag full of new clothes.
